claim_boundary: This is a deterministic NO_GO convergence and handoff inventory. It does not convert required local commands, open implementation prerequisites, or NOT_RUN external gates into passing evidence.

external_pre_release_handoff.evidence_required: live wrong-principal/entity/plane, validity, rotation, revocation, ACL, and downgrade negatives bound to one artifact set
two installed non-Rust implementations exercising the live transport without Rust decision FFI
delay/loss/duplication/reordering/partition, router/peer restart, slow-consumer/flood, and duration receipts
coverage-guided parser/state/FFI fuzzing plus sanitizers for the required duration and exact source
artifact-bound latency, throughput, memory, queue, and saturation profiles on declared platforms
clean installs and zero-skip conformance for crates, wheel/sdist, npm, and C/C++ artifacts
verified control of every final package name or one coordinated rename across manifests, docs, tests, and consumers
native installed-artifact certification for every enumerated consumer against the same NCP source and artifacts
clean-room source, package, core-conformance, and checksum reproduction without the originating build cache
verified package checksums, vulnerability/license dossier, SBOM/provenance attestations, signatures, and revocation procedure

repository_owned_open_prerequisites.acceptance: Implement an adapter that supplies callback-visible verified peer identity, bind every ingress identity/plane to it, and pass local negative tests before any live campaign.
repository_owned_open_prerequisites.current_behavior: ZenohBus::open_secure validates the strict config and refuses to open.
repository_owned_open_prerequisites.reason: Zenoh 1.9 callbacks do not identify the authenticated transport link/principal for each delivered sample or query.

repository_owned_open_prerequisites.acceptance: Upgrade to a reviewed Zenoh graph that resolves a patched lz4_flex, remove the advisory disposition, and pass the dependency exposure and current RustSec gates.
repository_owned_open_prerequisites.current_behavior: Transport compression stays disabled and the resolved feature graph is guarded, but stable publication remains blocked.
repository_owned_open_prerequisites.reason: Zenoh 1.9.0 retains lz4_flex 0.10.0 under RUSTSEC-2026-0041; no patched 0.10 release exists.

repository_owned_open_prerequisites.acceptance: Specify and review one bounded domain-separated result projection, implement independent Rust and TypeScript computation, add cross-language vectors, and rebaseline every affected normative artifact together.
repository_owned_open_prerequisites.current_behavior: Clients validate digest syntax and receipt correlation but do not claim result-body certification.
repository_owned_open_prerequisites.reason: The receipt result_digest has no approved normative nonrecursive wire-result projection that independent clients can recompute.

doi_and_zenodo.finding: No DOI or Zenodo archive is assigned or created. Citation metadata remains author-only candidate metadata until a later publication step assigns those identifiers.

historical_tag_and_release_cleanup.finding: No erroneous candidate tag or disposable release object exists. Historical annotated tags and release objects are compatibility, migration, and provenance evidence; v0.5.0 through v0.8.0 are machine-bound by released-baseline checks.

requested_0_9_release.finding: Current bytes are package 1.0.0-rc.1, wire 1.0, proto ncp.v1, and compact hash 163acc57d8a62b66. A v0.9 label would be incoherent; a genuine 0.9 requires a reviewed normative rebaseline and coordinated consumer migration.
